channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 60 replies — 203.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:53 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

Early replies (first 3)

62401710
Wow I can’t wait for this to not mean anything.

62401722
p+c etc.
don't care until the needle moves on this FUCKING GODDAMN TOKEN

62401738
>>62401704
xrp sneeded?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409640
>>62409606
>>62409613

62409661
>>62409640
How i feel in this thread! Meanwhile, I got the picture I am attaching to this comment in my back pocket, (which none of them know) just waiting to pull it out like the ultimate trump card. (¬‿ ͡¬) Dare I?

62409666
>>62406112
I don't know, but I sipped more link

62409669
>>62409666
>>62409583

62409675
>mB3FqHQS

62409679
>>62409675
Who is this? He looks crazy and scared. Is this from a movie or something? I don't recognize it. TL;DR?

62409681
>>62409675
I just saw you named the picture xrp. Are you saying this is XRP realizing the severity of what I posted in "62409661?" If so, post it again, because you are surely right!

62409739
i don't care anymore. they could do away with the token and send it to 0 and I would just post a LINK and picrel crossover thread to laugh at the copers who will be forever employed and get on with the rest of my life as if nothing happened and i didn't spend thkusands of hours reseaeching bullshit and making rarely reposted link memes for 7 years.
fuck you saturn

62409752
>>62409661
bless the undeserving, king

62409768
>>62409739
do things like that really happen though? No watchers, no justice? the whole token just poof?

62409835
>>62409752
*drops shades revealing the same knowing look from before, but sharper, and slams the picture down on table* BOOOOOM!

62409844
>>62409835

62409848
>>62409844
Exactly!

62409884
>>62409768
the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes, you can incentivize everyone with other assets, staking has obviously been unnecessary to secure value etc. it's "happened" in all but name. but it might work out anyway, idk.

62409907
price

62410035
>>62409884
>the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes
Same is true for Bitcoin, ETH, ...
Bitcoin was arguably way more secure when it was literal cents, and random faggots ran full mining nodes on their home PCs.

62410246
>mB3FqHQS
I've said it before and I'll say it again: the BOOOOOM meme absolutely destroyed some xerpies on here. Can't believe that after all the abuse and bullying they took for 10 years, simply posting the catchphrase of one of their twitter schizos is what did them in. Praying for you crazy bastards.

62411245
>>62410035
>duuuude it's not fair, bitcoin did xyz...

so how does it work in your head? 40 million altcoins should all have bitcoin or ethereum type price runup so things can become... fair?

you see folks, this is how the logic of a chainlink boyscout operates. no understanding that there isn't enough liquidity for their special altcoin to run like early crypto's did back when there was only like 50 of them. no understanding that obviously they aren't early anymore and therefore the days of those speculative runs are over.

instead of accepting the market they have, they cry like a child complaining to their parents that the other kids were allowed to have cookies before dinner.

they are: the few, the retarded... THE Cuckolds of crypto

62411677
>>62410035
i don't think the same is true for btc and eth. their ongoing function and adoption (by miners) depended on value realizable by the token. Link apparently doesn't have the same restriction.

62411795
>>62411677
>i don't think the same is true for btc and eth
Not only is what I said true for btc and eth, but both operated beautifully for a long time at a value of literally cents, proving that what I said is true for btc and eth.

>>62411245
>BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, ADA, TRX, Link, ... don't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes
>but ONLY Link cannot pump because of this reason

How does that work in your head?

62413787
>>62411795
at no point were eth or eth block rewards worth cents. btc, fair enough point, but you will concede that further adoption and miner buildout since that brief time has happened almost exclusively because of future price.
the same is not true for link, where price isn't the product or even a feature of its ongoing adoption.

62413804
>>62410246
I don't understand. How did the meme destroy them? I thought it was just us doing a sort of thing like the Costco Guys thing to have a sort of war cry and celebration chant over our success when news came out about link? That is the only context I have seen the meme so far here.

62413805
>>62401710
The future is now

62414082
>>62413805
What if we've always been living in the future as alien avatars and we traveled back in time to give Chainlink to ourselves (people say this about the small grey aliens a lot that they are like bio robots controlled remotely, what if they were implanted with our incarnated spirits?) and so the future was always? Brain BLOWN!

62415086
>>62413787
>at no point were eth or eth block rewards worth cents
lmao, this is explicitly about the price of the token/coin, not the value of mining rewards.

>further adoption and miner buildout since that brief time has happened almost exclusively because of future price
>link, where price isn't the product or even a feature of its ongoing adoption
We were talking about the "technical purposes" of the blockchain.
The technical purposes of Bitcoin and ETH were fully met when the price of both was merely cents.

You're talking about speculation, which is the same for any crypto.

62415177
>i4/u3PPu
>h/d99FjL
>the moronic shill who accuses everyone for samefagging is a samefag himself
ironic
notice how not one shill will even mention the AMA

62415187
>>62415177
>the AMA
humiliation ritual
>Hey guys, we know you've got a lot of burning questions about the project so we're hosting an AMA for you
>*top questions are about staking, tokenomics, and the function of the Reserve*
>ok, now that you've tuned in I will proceed to tell you about my favorite dApps and my opinion on using crypto in restaurants
>well, time's up now... thanks for tuning in!

62415313
>>62415086
ah i see you do not want to talk about the role of price and future value in sustaining the "technical purpose" of the network because nascent BTC was briefly worth pennies. also eth at no point traded for less than a few dollars once the network was producing blocks.

62415322
>>62415313
The price of ETH or BTC has pretty much no bearing on the technical purpose of the network. See the fact that both networks worked perfectly fine when the price was extremely low.
You're thinking of speculation.

62417648
Why is cll stealing staking rewards?

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 88 replies — 89.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:12 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62411638
>>62395915
Institutions are getting onboard, It might do 10X in a decade, also lending is starting to become more relevant with the likes of TBV, there are so many utilities untapped.

62412797
>>62411160
go to work

62413082
>>62411638
That's the part most people miss. BTC doesn't have to just sit in a wallet forever.

62413193
>>62406783
YOU'RE RETARD

62414853
>>62409813
Good idea

62414893
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
because there is this one nigger saylormoon who hold nearly a million of these, only boughted with debts & baggies hopes and dreams. MSTR alone was most of the buying pressure the past 2 years. What would the price look like to your opinion if it wasn't for Mike buying more than all the BTC mined worldwide each year?
We would already be back in the 15k range if it wasn't for him throwing infinite monies at BTC. And now his preferential dividend model is failing, do you really believe MSTR will be able to continue the purchase frenzy?
In just 6 months STRC went from 7 years worth of dividends to pay in their treasury down to 13 months. This boat is bleeding cash left and right.

62414918
>>62409051
Stolen from Sam Hyde

62414938
>>62414893
He knows what's he's doing

62414950
>>62413082
It won't work forever

62414955
>>62414944
How?

62415214
>>62414950
Hmmm, that's why it's still in public testnet instead of being pushed straight into production. I'm more interested in where it could lead than writing it off this early.

62415217
>>62414893
MSTR has definitely been a major buyer, but I don't think Bitcoin's entire value proposition depends on one company continuing to buy forever.

62415228
>>62395655
Because I have no brown, black or asian admixture.

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

item.title: STRC-FUCK IS GOING TO ZERO Z E R O — 34 replies — 86.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
STRC-FUCK IS GOING TO ZERO Z E R O

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:47 UTC

OP

STRC-FUCK IS GOING TO ZERO

Z E R O

Early replies (first 3)

62411204
>>62411180
$1 is fair value

62411235
bitFUCK

62414316
WTFWT

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414345
>>62414329
STRC will never return to par

62414350
>>62414345
but that would spell a disaster for microstrategy, do you understand the implication of bitcoin's biggest holder being in trouble?

62414374
>>62414350
Yes, and it makes my cock DIAMONDS.
We will get sub $40k BTC.

62414382
>>62411180
my penis will become hard, if it does.

62414429
>>62414350
>do you understand the implication of bitcoin's biggest bagholder being in trouble?
they're already in trouble

62414447
>>62414350
i thought satoshi was crypto's biggest holder. or do you mean biggest bagholder? because saylor is definitely that if his average is above 60k.
did he really think he could prop it up on his own or did he expect people to buy in? it hasn't even been a year since ath. is he nuts?

62414482
>>62414429
you sure about that?

62414497
>>62414482
who gives a fuck about dividend coverage
what do you think is going to happen when his creditors execute the put option in september and he doesn't have cash to cover the debt

62414503
>>62414497
it's called a refi

62414519
>>62414503
REFINANCE WHERE MICHAEL?

62414576
>mstr current debt $6.7 billion
>mstr current btc assets $50 billion
What is the concern here? Just stop paying dividends on the preferred shares and they'll be fine.

62414608
>>62411180
>someone bought MSTR at $195
>six weeks ago

62414616
>>62414519
just borrow money at 0% interest, not that fucking hard

62414621
>>62414616
BORROW MONEY WHERE, MICHAEL?

62414628
>>62414621
friends and family of course

62414641
>>62414621
Silicon Valley Bank

62414787
>>62411180
BASED

62414790
>>62414621
he just needs to sell a few chairs and she'll be right

62414804
>>62414519
>>62414621
from SBLOC (JPM, GS, BOFA) at 4%

62414808
>>62411180
I BOUGHT AT 100
AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH

62414819
>>62414576
this is the TORtanic all over again
because one ponzi scheme failed (LUNA)
every other financial product must implode in a cataclysmic event... because they have to... ok?
nevermind selling investment grade bonds at 4% and making use of SBLOC at 6... saylor will mint 10000000000 billion STRC to pay for STRC yield... because... idk

62414840
>>62414616

62414845
isn't saylor a billionaire? why doesn't he just donate some money to the cause

62414848
>>62414819
The funniest thing ever is that LUNC is outperforming link since Trump revived crypto. That is how down bad linkies are.

62414850
>>62414482
What happens to that number if BTC crashes alongside and synergistic with the stock, retard?

62414871
>>62414848
eth amazingly has performed even worse than both. what a disaster

62414969
>>62414871
I made a mistake using marketcap for a few. ETH it turns out is beating LUNC by HALF A PERCENT. And link is doing even worse! Lol

62417538
BOOOOOOM
SO BULLISH XRP

62417590
>>62411180
it's going to $100 with a 47% yield

62417624
>>62414482
>saylor would never fudge the numbers again like he did in the dotcom era

item.title: HOLY SHIT JUST PUMP SO I CAN SELL EVERYTHING AND QUIT I AM NEVER EVER *EVER* GONNA TOUCH YOU AGAIN fucking hell this ain… — 21 replies — 63.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOLY SHIT JUST PUMP SO I CAN SELL EVERYTHING AND QUIT I AM NEVER EVER *EVER* GONNA TOUCH YOU AGAIN fucking hell this ain…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:51 UTC

OP

HOLY SHIT JUST PUMP SO I CAN SELL EVERYTHING AND QUIT
I AM NEVER EVER *EVER* GONNA TOUCH YOU AGAIN

fucking hell this ain't for me. I should've put my money somewhere else. this crypto shit moves too fast. I could've sold at 82k, hell, I even predicted that it was gonna go up when I bought at 75k, but shit happened and I forgot to sell.

it's so stupid.

I just wanna put my money somewhere I don't need to look every goddamn day and not get my anus financially and economically prolapsed. I just don't want idle money. is that too much to ask?

I heard good things on ETFs, are they as hassle-free as they sound? it sounds more stable than this shit

Early replies (first 3)

62414803
>>62414756
there is no "just pump" you dumb nigger the money you lost is gone and you're going to watch the rest go away too because you can't handle taking an L

62414815
>>62414803
no, I'm gonna wait it out because that's the only thing I can do. no point in selling on a loss

62414816
>>62414756
nigger dont fucking touch it. bottom seller

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414832
>>62414756
sell it all
to me
at a discount
never look back

62414852
>>62414756
Just sell. Cash out your gains today and dont look back. Invest in broad index fund ETFs

62414879
>>62414756
Diversify you dumb nigger. I have 150k in BTC and 1.3mil in S&P 500. If BTC drops 50% I barely notice

62414885
>>62414815
>no point in selling on a loss
>t. literally every bagholder in the history of bagholding to zero

62415692
>>62414756
You should have sold the last time it pumped, chud.

62415700
>>62414885
kek this

62415775
>>62414852
Sell one bubble at the bottom to buy another one at its high?

62415789
>>62415775

Are you sure this is the bottom though?

62415912
>>62414879
If btc gives up 2x you'd also kind of barely notice. I think its just these people want to get rich fast but we are way past that stage.

Meanwhile I get a half chub thinking about how my 500k in the s&p at worst goes up 5% every year (we have had a good decade though)

62415954
kek

62415962
>>62414756
If it pumps you won't sell, you'll wait for it to go higher and it will dump even lower

62415975
>>62415962
This

62415982
>>62415962
The tale of the average baggie.
>Coin dumps
"Eh I'll hold. No point selling at a loss."
>Coin pumps
"Eh I'll hold. It will pump even higher and THEN I'll sell."

62416377
You are all doomed if you're not into GME.

62416382
>>62414756
Dont underestimate the power of positive thinking. Stay strong, brother

62416389
>>62416382
Yeah while the market trades flat to eat your premiums. Then they stop when everyone catches on to the scam.

62417475
>>62416389
>It's not my faaaauuuult
You weren't nearly as clever as you thought you were. Next time remember that predictions are difficult to get right, especially about the future. Nobody is obliged to go along with your cunning plan to fleece them, it's quite ok to fleece you right back.

Also no crying in the casino.

62417643
>>62414885
Refusing to sell at a loss has worked out for me, many red lines eventually turned greens

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-26 20:08 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417516
Bullish.

62417517
>>62417511
Brad gargleballs is an even bigger scammer.

62417535
>>62417503
>Chainlink Labs is transitioning

62417540
>>62417517
All the major institutions offering xrp ETF’s don’t seem to think so

62417561
>>62417503
makes sense

62417568
>>62417540
Theyre selling a product and making money from it. Thats it.

62417574
The only actual FUD I’ve felt about Link in 8 years was when they brought in this BUILD program. I was never interested in any sort of incentives outside of the coin, and i had zero interest in staking. I was always of the mindset that Link will go to $2,000 per coin or whatever; what the fuck do I care about getting some asshole’s project coins and increasing my net worth by 2% with it mooning. Even staking rewards I don’t give a fuck about. Although in hindsight I probably should have staked to get the extra 10% in free coins over the last few years, but I would have unstaked and sold irregardless once the price of Link got to $500 or whatever

62417587
>>62417514
More like Chainlink is stealing your rewards and giving it to themselves.

62417599
>>62417568
Hahaha, exactly

62417733
The main reason this should sound an alarm for linkies is that there's no reason they couldn't say "actually we're shutting down staking" or "actually we're shutting down need for the $LINK token" at any time. What's actually stopping them from doing this?

item.title: Why does SPCX have such a hard floor at $150? — 24 replies — 26.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why does SPCX have such a hard floor at $150?

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:16 UTC

OP

Why does SPCX have such a hard floor at $150? Every time it gets under $151, like clockwork, someone buys

Early replies (first 3)

62412944
>>62412935
This has always been 100% a Man.

62413007
>>62412935
The person on this picture is transgender right? It has a penis right?

62413061
>>62412935
because vested interests require $150 to prop up a valuation of $1.5 trillion on a mountain of absolute fucking garbage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413074
>>62412944
>>62413007
No. Don't feel bad, many people have thought the same thing. Its not your fault tranners have gotten so hot over the past decade nce.

62413079
>>62412944
That man has the most fuckable face on internet.

62413083
>>62413074
This kid was like 2018

62413094
>>62412935
Those are the limit orders that didn't go through 2 weeks ago

62413098
>>62413094
Interesting, could you tell me more about that? So there are people who placed an order at $150 when it IPOd and then just left them there?

62413107
>>62413007
Uses her finger to penetrate

62413251
HIT OR MISS
I GUESS THEY NEVER MISS HUH?

62413351
>>62413098
>So there are people who placed an order at $150 when it IPOd and then just left them there?
Yes.

62413472
>>62413061
Possibly. But more likely it's >>62413094

62413536
So much tranny propaganda and trolls online that I never realized if OP pic is a man with a wig or a real qt pie woman.
TL:DR is that a man or not?

62413639
>>62413251
YOU GOT A BOYFRIEND, I BET HE DOESNT KISS YA
MUAH
I miss 2018

62413642
>>62413536
It’s a genuine woman. Though her hair looks like a wig, I will admit.

62414159
>>62413639
You'll say the same thing about 2026 when someone says 6-7 in 2035

62414373
>>62412935
Hey better than schwab
I heard my uncle saying earlier his broker bought that shit at $210 a share lol.

62414420
>>62412935
I recognize this girl from a /pol/ post...

FOUND IT

(see picrel)

62415943
>>62414420
The way I see it phrased that I adore is that liberals went from "If Trump is elected then there will be another holocaust where he kills all the Jews!" to "Zion Don keeps sucking schlomo cock and six million wasn't enough"
And it wasn't even a decade

62416981
>>62414420
Jews will come to Israel.
Parts of Gaza will never return to Hamas and gazans.
worth it in the long run.
worth it.
Same with Lebanon, any deal won't hold buffer zone will be stablished, I could even see potential for more.


But Jews need to go back to Israel, better now than later

62416996
>>62412935
There's only 5% public float. Just imagine the crash when this becomes 15% in August.

62417487
>>62416996
Yeah imagine the crash when the NASDAQ and Dow Jones based index funds are forced to buy twice a month, at a rate that is 3x the float. Haha just imagine.

62417497
where we at when the next Starship launch is again.. kind of successful... kind of not?

62417510
>>62412935
probably because it actually doesnt and youre a homosexual

item.title: Who uses Polkadot? — 10 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who uses Polkadot?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:41 UTC

OP

With the Cardano dApp ecosystem completely imploding from the TapTools closure and the SecondFi exploit, it’s completely obvious Substrate is the only architectural survivor.

Cardano-cels thought Haskell was safe, but asynchronous backing and Polkadot 2.0 Agile Coretime prove that modular shared security is the only way to scale without draining user wallets. Now that the DOT treasury is finally pivoting away from multi-million dollar logo rebranding and actually funding core developer infrastructure, the ecosystem metrics are completely undeniable.

Why aren't you loading up on DOT at these prices before the parachain coretime marketplace goes fully liquid? It's literally a mathematically guaranteed breakout.

Early replies (first 3)

62412459
>>62412453

I do use Polkadot everyday

Its on my autobuy

62412625
>>62412453
awaiting the next bridge exploit to completely drain the polkadot system yet again

62412713
>>62412625

This is impossible

Polkadot is one of the most secure cryptos

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412947
>>62412713
look up what happened to acala and moonbeam, the biggest parachains

62412986
>>62412947

1. Aftermath: Acala recovered relatively well with community/governance intervention. This remains the main notable incident for the project. No major new breaches reported since.

2. No direct security breach: Searches turn up no evidence of a core protocol hack or exploit on Moonbeam itself. It was tangentially involved in the Acala incident (e.g., as a destination for some swapped funds or via shared Polkadot ecosystem elements like XCM channels)

???

62413181
>>62412986
AI is such shit. Acala never fucking recovered, it has a marketcap under 1 mil and legitimately went to zero (picrel). At the same time you had Moonbeam get completely drained by the Nomad bridge hack, with the team insisting they did no wrong despite being responsible for selecting the bridge against the protests of their own community and the bridge being financially backed by people affiliated with the team.

62413320
Nobody cares dude. Security? Scalability? Who gives a shit, all crypto is heading to zero. Agile Coretime and asynchronous who-gives-a-fuck… do you still think it’s 2021? I don’t care how agile the coretime is, it’s all garbage. The sooner you realize that the better off you’ll be.

62415156
50 bucks

62415218
>>62412453
>asynchronous backing and Polkadot 2.0 Agile Coretime prove that modular shared security is the only way to scale without draining user wallets
Make sure you calibrate the plasma conduits and do a full scan of the Jeffries' tubes too.

The dumbest of retards fall for technobabble. You are unworthy of life for being so gullible.

62417601
Everyone uses dot
It’s the future of finance

item.title: AI BROS I DON'T FEEL SO GOOD — 29 replies — 18.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI BROS I DON'T FEEL SO GOOD

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-26 18:37 UTC

OP

AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHHHHHH IT'S ALL FALLING

item.title: >Polkadot reached its all-time high market capitalization of approximately $63 billion on November 4, 2021, driven by a… — 7 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Polkadot reached its all-time high market capitalization of approximately $63 billion on November 4, 2021, driven by a…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-26 19:40 UTC

OP

>Polkadot reached its all-time high market capitalization of approximately $63 billion on November 4, 2021, driven by a peak token price of $55.13.

>XRP marketcap today = 65 billion

Early replies (first 3)

62412982
>>62412967
Don't let them know what an inflationary asset is. Leave the bagholders alone.

62412996
>>62412967
as far as I know monero lets you make anonymous payments that are untraceable
what can "polkadot" do

62413008
>>62412982
>>62412996

Inflation was fixed in the Spring

Current Situation (as of June 2026)
Hard supply cap: 2.1 billion DOT (implemented via community referendum and runtime upgrade around March 14, 2026).

This replaced the previous uncapped model.

Inflation reduction: Annual new issuance dropped sharply (e.g., from ~120 million DOT to ~56–57 million DOT initially — a ~53% cut). The effective net inflation rate is now around ~3.1% (after burns from treasury, coretime sales, etc.), and it continues to decline.

"Pi Schedule" disinflation: New issuance reduces by ~13.14% of the remaining gap to the cap every two years. This creates a stepped, predictable decline toward near-zero inflation over decades (projected to approach the cap around 2160, with ~1.91B DOT by ~2040).

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413009
>>62412996
I'm actually hoping it makes me a ton of money to throw into xmr lmao

62413024
>>62412996

Monero is a double edged sword. Europe is going to phase them out next. Some countries have banned it entirely.

The point of crypto isn't being anonymous like some people think, the main point is to hold any other asset other than the global reserve currency (greenbacks)

Polkadot is going to be the first chain to have not fast, but IMMEDIATE resources from JAM

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

62415155

62417598

62414417

62414437

62414440

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414443

62414444
>>62414440
it's insanely overvalued and buoyed by companies that make no money

62414449
>>62414440
>>62414444
it's just profit-taking you dumb fucks. the economy is booming

62414461
>>62414444
>>62414449
It's just wild how when it seemed like WWIII was about to begin everything was booming and everyone was so excited and now that nothing happened everyone becomes depressed o_O.

What the hell is going on?

62414467
>>62414461
it's all media sensation. the US has minted more millionaires than ever, people are making fuckloads of wealth and jobs are plentiful.

62414487
>>62414413
didnt this happen last year

62414500
>>62414461
that's how it works at the top, it pumps when people think it will dump, then gigadumps when they get complacent and think things are fine

62414504

that's the reason

62414508
>>62414449
idk i'm starting to think it's all a shell game. sure someone is siphoning money from retail but these valuations don't make any sense. sneedisk at $2k? c'mon. that's not even remotely organic.

62414534
Only thing pumping is my left hand and even that’s gonna dump soon.

62414585
>>62414413
i full ported into wendy burger at 8.88 and even that's down it's supposed to be the new game stop what the fuck is happening

62414592
>>62414585

62414594

62414735

62414753

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-26 18:32 UTC

OP

I still can’t believe you stupid assholes spammed Nazi frog memes to shit up official chainlink stuff to where they had to specifically change shit up to make it out to be that they aren’t associated with 4chan.

It fucking Hitler himself bought into the ICO he wouldn’t have done anything nearly as gay as that. And it’s probably to the detriment of our investment. I hope they chain you faggots up at the yacht party and throw you overboard like in the movie Amistad.

Early replies (first 3)

62412604
Have they used that cube in any official marketing since May 2018? It’s incredible how that image pervades despite the rebrand over 8 years ago.

62412715
>>62412582
What are you, unironically, that you ask such questions? banker? 33rd degree mason? are we getting close?

62412742

62412925

62412931
>>62412582

62413578
>>62412582
kek does somebody have the "a white man with a vision" cap under sergey's post?
that was peak really, everything went downhill from there.

62413593
>>62412582
Imagine being a world class institution like SWIFT and your only social media engagement is having Pepe and Apu PFP's screaming about some shitcoin integration and all because you made the mistake of talking to Sergey. Everything that dude touches turns to SHIT.

62413975
>>62413593
>glad you're on board, the last social media guy left
>why did he leave?
>we uh have a frog problem

62414081
>>62412931
It was before that

62414117
While the token price being 7usd all these years later is extremely irrelevant despite what you may believe. What is absolutely not irrelevant is the fact that Sergey basically got away with grabbing millions+ of cash in a funny time where crypto has none of the traditional protections of a stock. Eventually it won't be allowed for such a thing to happen again.

They should consider themselves very lucky to be able to do what they do. There's no bullshit now about wah link could be a security if we even merely discuss the token economics... Nah no the discord AMA proved the bullshit they need to be slapped around a bit for not actually answering important questions.

You can't have both. They can't expect people to stake 45m link and then not ask about what's next. They are supremely lucky to be able to do what they do and they need to know it

62414387
>>62412604
Getting rid of the cube was the end of the coin.

Without that cube they lost Saturn's blessing.

62414492
shut the fuck up and line up for your vaccine incels

62414557
>>62414117

are they? seems like they're just one of SWIFT's slush projects
